module1.clean_text:
No: [ID] P: [PATIENT] [DATE] Tiaret
Tel No: [ID] | First hospitalization:
Entry date: [DATE] date: | Resident: Dr. Benkhalfa
Assistant: Dr Khelil
No: [ID] P: [PATIENT] [DATE] [ID] No: [ID] | Entry date: [DATE] date: [DATE] | Resident: Dr. Tamazirt
Assistant: Dr. Khelil
Rehospitalization form
Chief physician:
PrrDRALI O
Assistant masters:
Dr KHELIL
k.aminaleila@yahoo.fr
Dr OUSSALAH
dr.oussalah.m@gmail.com
Telephone:
+213 [PHONE] | This is the infant [PATIENT] aged [AGE] from and residing in Tiaret from a middle-aged non-cansanguineous couple followed in our department since [DATE] for congenital PID, admitted for his 9th bolus of corticosteroids. HISTORY:
Family:
Father: [AGE] years old, BES, smoker
Mother: [AGE] years old, BES
Siblings: only son
Inbreeding: no
Atopy: no
Chronic respiratory disease in the family: no
Personal:
Pregnancy: well monitored
Upper birth canal Retroplacental hematoma
Apgar: good. No neonatal resuscitation
Hospitalization at the age of [AGE] for respiratory distress
Emission of meconium within 24 hours
Breastfeeding: mixed since birth
Food diversification
Incomplete vaccination, only received BCG
Vitamin D received at 01 month
DPM: judged good
Socio-economic level: average
Personal atopy allergic rhinitis
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: Congenital PID. Associated diagnoses: none
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] Age of diagnosis: [AGE] and 21 [AGE] Attending physician: Dr. KHELIL. Diagnostic elements:
Clinic:
Chronic respiratory distress since the age of [AGE] Signs of chronic respiratory failure: chest deformation (keeled thorax). Paraclinic:
Chest CT done on 12/26/2024: showing a frosted glass appearance
A retractile alveolar filling mainly dorsoapical and apical of the right lower lobe of a bronchopneumopathy accompanied by a discreet compensatory emphysema of the anterior segments. Complications of the disease:
Child presented with an exacerbation due to pseudomonas areginosae during his first hospitalization on 01/09/2025. Chronic respiratory failure: chest deformity. Etiological assessment:
Chest CT scan done on 12/26/2024: showing a frosted glass appearance. Control chest CT made on 08/18/2025: thickening of the right posteromedial bronchial walls
Rereading done by Dr Rebib: Right posterior apical condensation with aberrant vascularization of a malformative appearance, persistence of hyperinflation of the right middle lobe with anterior development, no arguments in favor of PID
Serology (11/25/2024): HIV (Elisa): negative, HBS ag negative, HCV AC: negative
TPHA VDRL negative. Sweat test: 01/15/2025: sweat weight: 153 mg. Chlorine level: 08 mEq/l: negative. Electrophoresis of serum proteins on capillaries: 03/06/2025: returning without abnormalities. FAN anti-nuclear antibodies: negative. Abdominal ultrasound: done on 01/19/2025: returning without abnormalities. Cardiac ultrasound: done on: 01/23/2025: returned without abnormalities. Redone on 07/27/2025: without anomalies. Reason for hospitalization
The infant was admitted on 10/11/2025 for his 8th bolus of corticosteroids. Intermediate history:
Since his last visit, he presented with ear pain with incessant crying, which motivated the mother to consult, an otitis with a mucous plug was diagnosed, hence his placement on local treatment with good improvement.
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 12/21/2025
Anthropometric measurements: Weight = Weight: 8kg500 [ - 1.2DS ]
Size: 72 cm [-0.50 DS ]
PC: cm [+0.58 DS]
BMI: 13.8
P/T(Waterloo) = 84%
GOMEZ: 76%
General condition, conscious, T°: 36.4°
Skin exam: TCNC
Pleuropulmonary examination:
Fr resp. : c/min, SPO2: 97% ss AA
Pulmonary Auscultation: Clear
Thoracic keel deformity
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 102 bpm, no breathing or sounds
CRT<3sec, no sign of hypoperfusion. ENT examination:
Soft mobile right cervical ADP without signs of inflammation.
No ear discharge
Head and neck examination:
Special features: rounded forehead, hirsutism
ADP of bilateral scalps
Abdominal examination: Soft abdomen, Transit preserves
Genital examination: male morphotype, poorly developed testicles, loose phimosis, uncircumcised. Neurological examination: conscious infant 15/15 on the Glasgow score, no convulsions or abnormal movements, good axial and segmental tone, normotensive AF, good DPM
Examination of the skin and appendages:
No scalp or nail damage
Osteoarticular examination:
No limitation of movements
Vertebrae aligned. PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION:
Biology: FNS: GB: 8870 (PNN: 2546, LYMPH: 5260, mono: 816). HB: 13.6. Plq: 279000 GS: B+positive
Na: 138 K: 4. Cl: 103
CRP:negative. VS: 4/9
Urea: 0.21 Creatinine: 2.8 mg/l
Blood calcium: 101.2 / Blood phosphorus: 54.4
Celiac disease serology: negative
Radiology:
TTX: 12/21/2025 at the limit of normal
TOGD: (14/11/2025): without anomalies
Control chest CT made on 08/18/2025: thickening of the right posteromedial bronchial walls
Rereading done by Dr Rebib: Right posterior apical condensation with aberrant vascularization of a malformative appearance, persistence of hyperinflation of the right middle lobe with anterior development, no arguments in favor of PID
Given the clinical and radiological improvement in the infant's condition, in the file visit made on 12/22/2025 with Pr. DRALI and Dr. KHELIL, we decided to stop the boluses of corticosteroids and to explore in order to confirm or refute the diagnosis of PID
So, we asked:
Thoracic MRI angiography to better characterize aberrant malformative vascularization appearing on CT
Ph-metry
Genetic study with PID panel. Conclusion:
The infant Naak Mouad aged 13 months from and living in Tiaret, from a non-consanguineous marriage, followed in our department for probable congenital PID, admitted for clarification. Recommendations: Please recover
Thoracic MRI angiography
Ph-metry
Genetic study of PID
Chief physician:
PrrDRALI O
Assistant masters:
Dr KHELIL
k.aminaleila@yahoo.fr
Dr OUSSALAH
dr.oussalah.m@gmail.com
Telephone:
+213 41 734 893
